items.headline: Equinix Data Center Permitted at Great Oaks Boulevard
items.summary: A $138 million data center expansion receives its building permit, making it by far the costliest project in this batch. The facility supports the server infrastructure that underpins cloud computing and AI services for the Bay Area tech sector.

items.headline: Chick-fil-A Permitted for Renovation at Blossom Hill Road
items.summary: The restaurant at 1162 Blossom Hill Rd is cleared for a demolition and rebuild project costing $809,557, suggesting a full facility refresh rather than routine maintenance.

items.headline: Congregation Kol Emeth permitted to add glass above existing trellis
items.summary: The synagogue at 4175 Manuela Avenue received a permit to increase lot coverage by adding glass above a portion of an existing trellis. This is a minor modification to the religious facility.

items.headline: Agreement signed for Seeds of Peace Monument at Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: San José City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for the Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by artist Mario Chiodo, to be located at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The monument will become part of the city's public art collection at the park.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District budget approved for FY 2026-27
items.summary: Council took action on the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District's FY 2026-27 budget and annual assessment. The business improvement district funds maintenance and improvements in the commercial area along Tully Road.

items.headline: City Council updates Health and Safety Element of General Plan
items.summary: The City Council is updating the Health and Safety Element of Cupertino's General Plan (covering 2015–2040). The agenda does not provide specifics on what changes are being made or how they affect residents.

items.headline: City Council appoints new permanent City Manager
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager on May 19. This fills the city's top administrative position.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates urban water master plan and drought response
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed the Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. These documents set Sunnyvale's water supply strategy and emergency rationing protocols as the region faces ongoing drought conditions.
